title: Palaeoproteomics Protocol for Arid Environment Samples
author: Louise Le Meillour
materials: NH 4 HCO 3 200 mL 50 millimolar (mM) Weigh 0.7906 g of ammonium bicarbonate (Sigma Aldrich). Add the weighed powder to 100 mL of milliQ water. Shake manually until the crystals are dissolved. Make up to 200 mL. Store at room temperature.
MilliQ Water 1 mL Acetic Acid 2.8 µL
Thermomixer 450 rpm, 50 °C, 1 hour Allow the samples to come to room temperature (on the bench, approx. 30 min)
ThermoMixer 350 rpm, 37 °C, 18 hours, Trypsin can act in only 3 to 5h

steps.title: EDTA 500 mL 0
steps.instruction: EDTA 500 mL 0.5 Molarity (M) pH 7.4 Under a fume hood pH meter in the solution and bar magnet

steps.title: Iodoacetamide solution 1 mL 1 Molarity (M) Weigh 184
steps.instruction: Iodoacetamide solution 1 mL 1 Molarity (M) Weigh 184.9 mg of iodoacetamide and add to 1 mL of milliQ water in a tube covered with foil or brown microtube. Shake manually until the crystals are dissolved. Can be stored at -20°C.

steps.title: MilliQ water + Acetic acid 100...
steps.instruction: MilliQ water + Acetic acid 100 µL Trypsin 100 µg Mix in the tube by ups and downs

steps.title: Divide into pellets 10 µL Store...
steps.instruction: Divide into pellets 10 µL Store -20 °C Do not thaw the trypsin more than 3 times

steps.title: Prepare paper and EtOH for cleaning between samples
steps.instruction: Prepare paper and EtOH for cleaning between samples

steps.title: Use ultrasounds to clean diamond head...
steps.instruction: Use ultrasounds to clean diamond head of dremel in the end

steps.title: Add EDTA to each sample 1...
steps.instruction: Add EDTA to each sample 1 mL Store 4 °C Put on a mixing carousel to allow contact with every “particle” of bone/tooth

steps.title: Change the solution once
steps.instruction: Change the solution once. Centrifuge 13400 x g, °C, 1 minute Collect the supernatants in a tube labelled with the sample code. Homogenise the mixture (Vortex 1min) Store 4 °C Decalcification is completed when only a bone phantom remains (should resemble wet cotton candy)

steps.title: Add ammonium bicarbonate to each sample...
steps.instruction: Add ammonium bicarbonate to each sample 500-300 µL 50 Molarity (M)

steps.title: For every 100µL of sample, add...
steps.instruction: For every 100µL of sample, add DTT 1 µL 1 Molarity (M) (Final concentration ~10mM)

steps.title: Add Iodoacetamide solution 1
steps.instruction: Add Iodoacetamide solution 1.6 µL 1 Molarity (M) (final concentration approx. 15mM) Incubate for 30 min in the dark (protected by foil)

steps.title: Add 1 µL of trypsin prepared...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of trypsin prepared at 1 µg/µL to 300 μL of solubilisation solution

steps.title: Prepare the formic acid which will...
steps.instruction: Prepare the formic acid which will be used to stop the digestion: Pure formic acid 10 µL MilliQ water 90 µL Adjust the volume to be prepared according to the number of samples

steps.title: Stop the digestion by adding 1...
steps.instruction: Stop the digestion by adding 1 µL of prepared 10% formic acid.

steps.title: Place between 40 and 100 μL...
steps.instruction: Place between 40 and 100 μL of each sample in an insert dedicated to the LC-MS/MS analyses with an electrospray source.
